Title: cis-Golgi phosphate transporters harboring an EXS domain are essential for plant growth and development

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: apoplastic space; Golgi-derived vesicles/Golgi lumen context
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential nutrient; Golgi Pi homeostasis is required to support nucleotide-sugar transport, cell wall synthesis, protein glycosylation, plant growth, and fertility
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: EXS-domain-containing membrane protein; ERD1 family phosphate transporter
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broadly expressed in vegetative and reproductive tissues; root meristem and vascular cylinder, rosette leaf vasculature and lamina, stamen filament, pistil, and pollen grains; higher than ERD1B in roots and leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broad expression in roots, leaves, flowers, and pollen; enriched relative to ERD1B in roots and shoots/leaves
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ERD1A native promoter (complementation/GUS); CaMV35S constitutive promoter (overexpression)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Brefeldin A treatment abolished increased apoplastic Pi export in erd1a; dark-grown hypocotyl assay; variable external Pi supply did not rescue phenotype
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ERD1A is required for Golgi/apoplastic Pi homeostasis; loss of ERD1A increases apoplastic Pi export and impairs cell wall composition, hypocotyl/root growth, pollen germination, pollen tube elongation, and fertility
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERD1A encodes a cis-Golgi EXS-domain protein required for proper phosphate export from the Golgi. 2. erd1a mutants show elevated Pi export to the apoplast, dependent on vesicular trafficking, without major whole-plant Pi starvation signatures. 3. Loss of ERD1A alters cell wall monosaccharide composition and reduces shoot growth, hypocotyl extensibility, root elongation, pollen fitness, and male fertility. 4. Native-promoter ERD1A-GFP complements erd1a, while CaMV35S overexpression causes partial plasma membrane mislocalization and promotes apoplastic Pi export.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutant analysis; genetic complementation with pERD1A::ERD1A-GFP; overexpression; RT-qPCR; GUS reporter; confocal subcellular localization; 33Pi export assay; Brefeldin A sensitivity assay; cell wall monosaccharide profiling; pollen germination/tube growth assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering Golgi phosphate homeostasis and cell wall-associated growth/fertility traits, but manipulation requires caution because both loss and overexpression perturb Pi export and development
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout causes severe stunting and male sterility; overexpression leads to partial plasma membrane mislocalization and ectopic apoplastic Pi export
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ManI (cis-Golgi colocalization marker); ERD1B; CBL1 (plasma membrane colocalization marker in overexpression context)
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: cis-Golgi; partial plasma membrane localization upon strong overexpression
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Published RNA-seq data from Arabidopsis roots and leaves (Deforges et al. 2019; accession NA) used for expression comparison
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phenotype is distinct from PHT4;6. Increased apoplastic Pi export in overexpression lines is attributed to mislocalized ERD1A at the plasma membrane, whereas increased export in erd1a likely reflects excess Pi packaged into Golgi-derived vesicles due to defective Golgi Pi efflux.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports Golgi Pi homeostasis required for glycosylation, cell wall biosynthesis, and reproductive development
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: EXS-domain-containing membrane protein; ERD1 family phosphate transporter
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broad but weaker than ERD1A; seen in cotyledon, root, rosette leaf, and pistil; not detected in stamen and pollen under GUS conditions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower expression than ERD1A in roots and leaves; broad but weak expression
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ERD1B native promoter; ERD1A promoter; CaMV35S constitutive promoter
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ERD1B has similar biochemical function to ERD1A and can complement erd1a when expressed strongly enough, but erd1b single mutants show no obvious visible phenotype due to weaker expression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERD1B is a close ERD1A homolog localized to the cis-Golgi. 2. erd1b knockout alone shows no obvious growth phenotype. 3. ERD1B can partially or fully complement erd1a depending on promoter strength, indicating similar function to ERD1A. 4. Together with ERD1A, it is required for male gamete transmission because absence of both abolishes pollen transmission.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; transgenic complementation of erd1a with pERD1A::ERD1B-GFP and 35S::ERD1B-GFP; RT-qPCR; GUS reporter; confocal colocalization; yeast functional complementation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential backup/stacking target with ERD1A for modulating Golgi Pi homeostasis while avoiding complete loss-of-function defects
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined loss with ERD1A abolishes male transmission; weaker expression may limit utility unless strongly expressed
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Published RNA-seq data from Arabidopsis roots and leaves (Deforges et al. 2019; accession NA) used for expression comparison
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Lower transgene accumulation than comparable ERD1A constructs suggests possible post-transcriptional regulation. erd1a erd1b double deficiency is male gametophyte lethal or non-transmissible.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains Golgi Pi homeostasis linked to mannan-rich cell wall biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native ScERD1 promoter; GPD/TDH3 strong constitutive promoter in p415/p425 vectors
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low-Pi growth medium used for Pi export assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ScERD1 restores the yeast erd1 mutant defects in cell wall mannan and excess Pi export; overexpression in WT increases extracellular Pi export
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Native ScERD1 complements yeast erd1 defects in Alcian blue staining, mannan content, and excess Pi export. 2. Strong overexpression of ScERD1 in WT yeast increases extracellular Pi export. 3. These data support ERD1 proteins as Golgi Pi exporters.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast mutant complementation; promoter-driven expression; Alcian blue staining; cell wall polysaccharide analysis; 33Pi export assay; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily mechanistic reference gene validating ERD1 transporter function across species
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis ERD1A/B complemented Pi-export and cell wall phenotypes of yeast erd1, supporting conserved transporter function.
